headline: Bernard Arnault, generous patron of the arts and lover of tax breaks
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (5/6). France's richest man is also one of Europe's leading art collectors. Through the Fondation Louis Vuitton, he combines his passion for the arts with highly lucrative tax arrangements.

headline: US vows to punish Iran after Houthis strike tankers
US unveils new tariffs on 60 partners as Trump rebuilds trade agenda
After two diplomats were violently detained in Tehran, France expresses concern for its nationals in Iran
description: One of the two staff members from the French embassy in Tehran who were detained on Sunday was 'manhandled,' 'brutalized' and 'beaten,' said Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot. As the diplomats returned to Paris on Wednesday, French authorities fear that the Islamic Republic might resume its practice of taking hostages.

headline: Bruno Cotte, magistrate: 'Contrary to what Marco Rubio claims, the International Criminal Court is neither right-wing nor left-wing'
description: The US Secretary of State's campaign to dismantle the ICC marks an assault on international law that should prompt much stronger reactions from its founders, especially the Europeans, argues the former ICC chamber president for Le Monde.

headline: In Bastia, the 'Corsica-Maghreb' exhibition explores the migratory connections between the island and North Africa
description: The city's museum offers a fascinating look at the intertwined histories of Corsica and North Africa, featuring 336 works, objects and documents.

headline: The mysterious horse-shaped geoglyph England has been maintaining for thousands of years
description: The Uffington White Horse is one of the best known geoglyphs in the UK. Drawn around 3,000 years ago on a hillside in Oxfordshire, its chalk outline is maintained each year by volunteers through a restoration campaign.

How Macron narrowly averted his environment minister's resignation
description: Monique Barbut, who said she intended to quit the government on Tuesday, July 21, after an agricultural law she contested was adopted, ended up staying on in her role on Wednesday. The twist added a colorful note to the political crisis.

headline: Zelensky forced to dismiss Oleksandr Syrsky, Ukraine's armed forces chief
description: Yielding to mounting pressure from demonstrators calling for General Syrsky's departure, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky reversed course and has replaced him with General Mykhailo Drapaty.

headline: Despite living 'like a walking dead man,' South Africa's Thabiso Zulu continues crusade against ANC corruption
description: For nearly a decade, the 44-year-old man has lived like a hunted animal. He sleeps in a different place almost every night and only shares meeting locations at the last moment.
